intent: Forward the CLI --regex-pattern argument that argparse accepted but slugify_params dropped; the patch adds regex_pattern to the command params default map and adds TestCommandParams.test_regex_pattern that asserts both the params mapping and the resulting slugify() output.
notes:
Phase 5.8 case_004 selected by cgpro session phase58-soak (conversation 69ef3a8c-0198-8394-8f09-14a7b120d192) targeting un33k/python-slugify@7edf477 — 'Fix --regex-pattern being ignored by the CLI' (fixes #175, author Jacobo de Vera). Strategy: target_install=true (editable install required for console_scripts and parse_args + slugify_params imports). claim_id=C.python_slugify.cli_regex_pattern_forwarded, claim_type=precondition_supported (in the verifier's VerifierClaimType Literal), pytest_scope=test.py.

Pre-dispatch local gate: git clone https://github.com/un33k/python-slugify, git checkout 7edf477f64b65ffe22c966d6a8dcc3edd0fb6997, pip install -e ., pytest test.py → 83 passed in 0.10s; pytest test.py::TestCommandParams::test_regex_pattern (the new regression test) PASSES. oida-code verify-grounded --repo-root /tmp/python-slugify-case004 against the bundle → status=verification_candidate, accepted_claims=[C.python_slugify.cli_regex_pattern_forwarded], pytest status=ok, [E.tool.pytest.0] kind=test_result with the new pytest_summary_line='83 passed in 0.08s' field populated and folded into evidence_items[0].summary.

case_004 also surfaced an adapter bug (commit c7734b3): python-slugify's pyproject.toml pins addopts='--color=yes' so pytest emits ANSI SGR escapes even through subprocess pipes; the Phase 5.8.x parser was matching the decorated line and returning None. Fix: strip CSI/SGR escapes via _ANSI_ESCAPE_RE before applying the canonical regex. Two new regression tests cover (a) colored '83 passed in 0.08s' line and (b) the dual-backend trap signal '24 passed, 5 skipped' surviving ANSI decoration. Test count 958 → 960 (+2). Committed before dispatching the workflow so the run uses the fixed parser.

Workflow run 25050370380 (success): cross-repo checkout un33k/python-slugify@7edf477 → oida-target/, editable install via inputs.target-install=true, pytest from oida-target/ cwd against scope test.py → status=ok, [E.tool.pytest.0] kind=test_result with pytest_summary_line='83 passed in 0.07s'. Verifier accepted C.python_slugify.cli_regex_pattern_forwarded with status=verification_candidate. enriched_evidence_refs=[[E.tool.pytest.0]]. gateway-official-field-leak-count=0. ADR-22 hard wall preserved.

cgpro session phase58-soak (conversation 69ef3a8c-0198-8394-8f09-14a7b120d192) labelled useful_true_positive UX 2/2/2/2 — first case dispatched AFTER the Phase 5.8.x evidence-shape upgrade (and ANSI fix), so all four UX axes scored 2 from the first pass. cgpro rationale: 'gateway accepted ... with no rejected or unsupported claims and no ADR-22 leak ... evidence summary now includes the concrete terminal result "83 passed in 0.07s" so the operator can see the scoped test file completed cleanly without needing to infer the clean pass from adapter behavior'.

biggest_trap (cgpro pre-pick): PR-branch SHA must be pinned exactly (PR was open at pick time). False promotion would happen if the verifier observes generic slugify tests pass without grounding the CLI forwarding precondition specifically.
